Introduction.script: The paper introduces the Transformer as a sequence model built around attention instead of recurrence or convolution.
bullet_points: The goal is efficient sequence transduction such as machine translation.

Methodology.script: The architecture uses stacked encoder and decoder layers with multi-head self-attention, feed-forward networks, residual connections, and positional encodings.
bullet_points: Scaled dot-product attention is the core operation.
Positional encodings provide token order information.

Results.script: The Transformer reports strong BLEU scores on WMT 2014 English-to-German and English-to-French translation tasks.
bullet_points: The base model reaches 27.3 BLEU on English-to-German.
The big model reaches 28.4 BLEU on English-to-German and 41.8 BLEU on English-to-French.

Discussion.script: The paper argues that attention-based modeling improves parallelization and handles long-range dependencies effectively.

Conclusion.script: The work shows that attention alone can support state-of-the-art sequence modeling and becomes a foundation for later architectures.
bullet_points: The Transformer changes how sequence models are designed.
